purpose: Defines the `!cancel [dashboardId] [--force]` command for immediate swarm cancellation. Marks all non-completed agents as failed via progress file writes — overwrites in-progress files to status:failed and creates failed progress files for pending tasks. Without `--force`, requires user confirmation via a `permission` log entry that triggers a dashboard popup. Produces overwritten/created progress files plus a final logs.json entry summarizing completed-vs-cancelled counts.
gotchas: Running agents may CONTINUE in the background after cancellation — their progress file writes will succeed (the files exist) but the swarm is finalized; only restarting the Claude Code session truly stops them
This is THE one documented exception where the master writes progress files — cancellation is a forced state override
In-progress progress files are overwritten with status:failed and a 'may still be running' summary — the worker's actual work may still complete in the background
Without --force, the master MUST write a `permission` log entry before asking in the terminal (triggers dashboard popup)
Completed progress files are never modified — completed work is always preserved
patterns: 7-step procedure: parse args+flag → read init → read progress → confirm (or skip if --force) → write progress files for non-completed → log cancellation → display summary
Three-state cancel logic: completed→leave, in_progress→overwrite as failed, no file→create failed progress file
Permission popup pattern: `permission` log entry triggers dashboard alert before terminal question
conventions: Syntax block lists every variant including --force combinations
Inline note callout warning that running agents may continue
JSON code blocks for log entry and progress file shapes
